    PSM for coral Sr/Ca¶

    In this tutorial, we introduce the PSM for coral Sr/Ca in cfr.

    In [1]:
    Copied! 
    %load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2

import cfr
print(cfr.__version__)
    %load_ext autoreload %autoreload 2 import cfr print(cfr.__version__)

    Data preparation¶

    Proxy¶

    In [2]:
    Copied! 
    pdb = cfr.ProxyDatabase().fetch('PAGES2kv2')
    pdb = cfr.ProxyDatabase().fetch('PAGES2kv2')
    In [3]:
    Copied! 
    pobj = pdb['Ocn_061']
fig, ax = pobj.plot()
    pobj = pdb['Ocn_061'] fig, ax = pobj.plot()

    Model¶

    In [4]:
    Copied! 
    # note here the nc file has been interpolated to the atmos grid

%%time
dirpath = './data/icesm_last_millennium_historical'
model_tos = cfr.ClimateField().load_nc(os.path.join(dirpath, 'tos_sfc_Omon_iCESM_past1000historical_085001-200512_interp.nc'))
    # note here the nc file has been interpolated to the atmos grid %%time dirpath = './data/icesm_last_millennium_historical' model_tos = cfr.ClimateField().load_nc(os.path.join(dirpath, 'tos_sfc_Omon_iCESM_past1000historical_085001-200512_interp.nc'))
    CPU times: user 980 ms, sys: 7.35 ms, total: 987 ms
Wall time: 1.05 s

    Get climate data for a specific ProxyRecord¶

    In [5]:
    Copied! 
    %%time

pobj.del_clim()
pobj.get_clim(model_tos, tag='model')
    %%time pobj.del_clim() pobj.get_clim(model_tos, tag='model')
    CPU times: user 208 ms, sys: 3.1 s, total: 3.31 s
Wall time: 1min 24s

    Create a PSM object¶

    In [6]:
    Copied! 
    mdl = cfr.psm.Coral_SrCa(pobj)
    mdl = cfr.psm.Coral_SrCa(pobj)
    In [7]:
    Copied! 
    %%time
pp = mdl.forward()
    %%time pp = mdl.forward()
    CPU times: user 498 µs, sys: 537 µs, total: 1.03 ms
Wall time: 1.35 ms
    In [8]:
    Copied! 
    fig, ax = pp.plot()
    fig, ax = pp.plot()
